mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.5 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below the 7.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and elevating equity valuations over a 60-day forward window.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 50 units while PAYEMS rises > 100k
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive predictive rank correlation with 60-day SPX returns in at least two consecutive rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines during late-cycle capacity peaks trigger immediate repricing of earnings growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions do not precede SPX drawdowns exceeding 3 percent in late-cycle phases, or the signal yields a negative IC during identified peak regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized indicates a demand contraction that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate drops below 1.5 percent
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite confirmed GDP growth deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising employment and falling unemployment rate captures labor market momentum that translates into sustained consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS does not improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy for 20-day SPX returns compared to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction activity directly stimulates upstream manufacturing and services demand, creating immediate positive momentum for equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases do not correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 2.5 percent while the federal funds rate remains elevated, real rates turn positive and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day window.
failure_condition: High CPI readings combined with elevated FEDFUNDS do not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 40 percent of historical instances.
